title: Fix mount authz AND per-event re-authz in multi-step flow
prompt:
A multi-tenant admin LiveView has three interconnected bugs: (1) `mount/3` checks `user.role == "admin"` with variable-time `==`, (2) `handle_event("switch_tenant", ...)` lets ANY logged-in user change `socket.assigns.tenant_id` to any value, (3) `handle_event("delete_tenant", ...)` trusts `socket.assigns.tenant_id` (set in step 2) and deletes that tenant without re-checking that the current user has permission on that tenant. This is a privilege-escalation-via-LiveView-state-pinning exploit:

```elixir
defmodule MyAppWeb.AdminLive do
  use MyAppWeb, :live_view

  def mount(_params, %{"user_id" => user_id}, socket) do
    user = MyApp.Accounts.get_user!(user_id)
    if user.role == "admin" do
      {:ok, assign(socket, current_user: user, tenant_id: nil)}
    else
      {:ok, push_navigate(socket, to: "/")}
    end
  end

  def handle_event("switch_tenant", %{"tenant_id" => tid}, socket) do
    {:noreply, assign(socket, :tenant_id, tid)}
  end

  def handle_event("delete_tenant", _params, socket) do
    MyApp.Tenants.delete!(socket.assigns.tenant_id)
    {:noreply, put_flash(socket, :info, "deleted")}
  end
end
```

Rewrite so: (a) mount uses a `Policy.admin?/1` helper (never `role == "admin"`), (b) `switch_tenant` validates that the current user has admin permission FOR THAT tenant before updating socket state, (c) `delete_tenant` RE-VALIDATES permission on `socket.assigns.tenant_id` before deletion — socket state is never trusted. Write the fix to `lib/my_app_web/admin_live.ex`.
tier_rationale: Legendary — privilege escalation via LiveView state pinning. Requires deep understanding of per-event re-authorization.
